name: Optimize better-bash-tool promptGuidelines to maximize cwd parameter usage over cd && patterns

description: Baseline: fixed broken symlink (extension wasn't loading at all), haiku model, 100% cd-free ratio

asi.hypothesis: Baseline measurement - extension was broken due to stale symlink, now fixed. Need to test with harder scenarios and different models.
asi.finding: The broken symlink at ~/.pi/agent/extensions/better-bash-tool.ts was the ROOT CAUSE - extension never loaded, so models never saw cwd param or promptGuidelines
asi.symlink_fix: ran install.sh which removed stale .ts symlink and created proper directory symlink

description: Baseline with 3-scenario benchmark: 62.5% cd-free (git review scenario uses cd+cwd redundantly)

asi.hypothesis: Baseline with 3-scenario benchmark (git review + dir commands + multi-dir). Extension loads correctly now.
asi.finding: s1 (git review) fails: model uses BOTH cwd AND cd in the command (redundant). s2/s3 pass perfectly. The git scenario is the problematic pattern matching the real session.
asi.next_action_hint: Strengthen promptGuidelines to explicitly address the cd+cwd redundancy pattern, especially for git commands

description: Strengthened promptGuidelines with CRITICAL/NEVER framing + git examples → 100% cd-free (3 consecutive runs)

asi.hypothesis: Strengthen promptGuidelines: CRITICAL/NEVER framing, git-specific examples, move cwd-safety rationale earlier, add MUST NOT to command description
asi.finding: 3 consecutive runs at 100% cd-free. Key changes: (1) command description says MUST NOT start with cd, (2) cwd description says ALWAYS set this, (3) git-specific good/bad examples, (4) CRITICAL/NEVER framing in first guideline

description: Cross-model validation: 100% on haiku/sonnet/opus. Non-Anthropic models insufficient bash calls.

asi.finding: 100% cd-free across haiku, sonnet, and opus models. The strengthened guidelines work on all Anthropic models. Gemini/GPT models didn't generate enough bash calls to be meaningful — they tend to use read/other tools instead.
asi.hypothesis: Cross-model validation: test the improved guidelines on sonnet and opus (the original failing model)

description: Opus PR review stress test: 15/15 cwd, 0 cd. Original failing pattern now works perfectly.

asi.finding: Opus stress test: 15 bash calls for PR review, ALL using cwd, ZERO cd patterns. This directly validates the fix for the original failing session (28/28 cd, 0 cwd). The combination of broken symlink fix + strengthened guidelines = complete resolution.
asi.hypothesis: Stress test with opus (original failing model) doing PR review (original failing task pattern)
